_comment: Example /etc/fleet/mcp-policy.json that lets an UNPRIVILEGED MCP client (e.g. Claude run as an ordinary user) deploy and manage app lifecycle through the privilege-separated root daemon — without sudo. read/mutate stay allowed; the destructive tier stays denied by default, and only the specific lifecycle tools are opted in per-tool. Every call is still rate-limited and written to /var/log/fleet-mcp/audit.log. Copy to /etc/fleet/mcp-policy.json and restart the daemon (sudo systemctl restart fleet-mcp).
